outreach.diagnosis: Dicki's New Farm already has a website, but it likely undersells the real business. The redesign opportunity is to improve clarity, trust, and the path from interest to booking or enquiry.
outreach.coldMessageAngle: I took a look at the current site and mocked up a version that makes the offer clearer and the next step easier for mobile visitors.

faq.question: What does Dicki's New Farm help with?
faq.answer: Dicki's New Farm can present its core Vegan restaurant services clearly here, with final wording replaced by owner-approved details.

findings.title: Core services are not instantly clear
findings.evidence: Expected services were not all visible in captured text: Vegan restaurant, Vegetarian restaurant.
findings.impact: A lead may not recognize that the business solves their specific problem.
findings.fix: Show 3-5 core services near the top with plain-language outcomes.

findings.title: Trust signal is not strong in the first screen
findings.evidence: The first captured text block does not clearly surface reviews, warranty, credentials, years, or local proof.
findings.impact: For local services, trust often matters before design polish.
findings.fix: Add a compact trust strip near the hero: reviews, warranty/licence claims when verified, service area, and response promise.

findings.title: Proof is not doing enough work
findings.evidence: The captured text does not clearly surface reviews, project proof, warranty, licences, or gallery proof.
findings.impact: The site may feel informational rather than convincing.
findings.fix: Add a proof section with real reviews, project examples, and verified guarantees/credentials.

findings.title: Title does not target the main local intent
findings.evidence: Captured title: "Dicki's New Farm". Inferred keyword: "restaurant Vegan restaurant".
findings.impact: The page may rank or click worse for the service/location query we care about.
findings.fix: Rewrite title around "restaurant Vegan restaurant" while keeping the business name.

findings.title: Meta description is missing
findings.evidence: No meta description was captured.
findings.impact: Search snippets may be generic or less compelling.
findings.fix: Write a specific 1-2 sentence snippet with service, location, and next action.

findings.title: H1 structure needs cleanup
findings.evidence: 0 H1 tag(s) captured.
findings.impact: A single clear H1 helps both search and page comprehension.
findings.fix: Use one H1 focused on the primary service/location promise.

findings.title: Page content is thin
findings.evidence: 28 words captured.
findings.impact: Thin pages have fewer chances to explain services, locations, and trust.
findings.fix: Add concise service, proof, area, and FAQ copy without stuffing.

findings.title: Image alt text is weak
findings.evidence: 3 image(s) missing alt attribute and 5 image(s) have empty alt.
findings.impact: Images do not help accessibility, local proof, or image-search context.
findings.fix: Add descriptive alt text for content images; keep decorative images empty intentionally.

findings.title: Structured data is missing
findings.evidence: No JSON-LD schema types were captured.
findings.impact: LocalBusiness schema can make core business facts clearer to search engines.
findings.fix: Add LocalBusiness/Organization schema with verified name, phone, area, URL, and social links.

findings.title: Basic trust/legal links are thin
findings.evidence: Privacy or terms links were not captured from visible page links.
findings.impact: This is a minor trust and SEO hygiene gap, especially when forms collect customer data.
findings.fix: Add privacy and terms links in the footer when forms or tracking are used.

findings.title: Internal linking is weak
findings.evidence: 0 internal link(s) captured.
findings.impact: Important pages may be harder to discover and preserve during redesign.
findings.fix: Link to services, contact/quote, about/proof, and relevant location pages.

priorityActions.title: Core services are not instantly clear
priorityActions.fix: Show 3-5 core services near the top with plain-language outcomes.

priorityActions.fix: Add a compact trust strip near the hero: reviews, warranty/licence claims when verified, service area, and response promise.

openDesignHandoffDraft.prompt:
Do not ask follow-up questions. Make reasonable assumptions from the payload and build the concept files now.
This is a pre-sale mockup, so complete the page with internally safe demo copy instead of waiting for missing minor details.

Build a one-page website for Dicki's New Farm, a Vegan restaurant in Brisbane.

Audience: Vegan restaurant presented with a cleaner first impression, faster trust, and a stronger path to enquiry.
Hero angle: Dicki's New Farm deserves a clearer Vegan restaurant presence online
Primary CTA: Get in touch
Visible contact detail: (07) 3254 2341.
All call buttons must use href="tel:0732542341" and visible text should include (07) 3254 2341.
Conversion goal: contact form lead; include a contact form routed through the existing Resend transactional email flow to hi@profitslocal.com.
Questionnaire answers: if your design workflow expects a questionnaire, use these answers automatically:
- Primary surface: Responsive - all sizes, mobile-first contact actions.
- Audience: Vegan restaurant presented with a cleaner first impression, faster trust, and a stronger path to enquiry.
- Visual tone: clear, human, locally credible
- Brand context: Pick a tasteful industry-appropriate direction; do not wait for a brand spec.
- CTA contact detail: (07) 3254 2341.
- Contact form fields: Name, Phone, Service needed, Message, Preferred call time.
Current site audit: 35/100. Use the audit findings to show clear uplift; if the site is already strong, keep the concept restrained.

Sections/pages to cover: hero, menu-or-offer, trust, about, cta.
Services to show: Vegan restaurant, Vegetarian restaurant, Signature offer, Private bookings, Visit information.
Trust signals: Google profile available, Before/after redesign clarity.

Use tasteful, conversion-focused design. Fill demo copy completely using common-sense industry best practices where verified details are missing.
Create the actual HTML/CSS/JS artifact. Do not return a question form, questionnaire, plan-only response, or request for more input.
Do not invent phone, email, address, awards, prices, certifications, medical/legal claims, or review quotes.
Avoid AI-looking gradients, generic stock photos, empty placeholder blocks, "Welcome to" headlines, and "Your trusted partner" copy.

buildPacket.nextAction: Use the autonomous website handoff for Open Design mockup generation, then replace demo-only content before production launch.
